content.content:
{"body":"## Summary
- Replace fragile manual YAML string concatenation in `nix/module.nix` with `builtins.toJSON`, eliminating indentation bugs like the recent telegram fields fix
- Add missing NixOS module options for radarr, prowlarr, overseerr, and notebook to match the Go config struct
- Use `lib.optionalAttrs` for all conditional config sections (telegram, radarr, prowlarr, overseerr, notebook)

## Details

The Go binary uses `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` which parses JSON natively (YAML is a superset of JSON), so no Go-side changes are needed. The config file extension stays as `config.yaml` for clarity.

New module options added:
- `radarrUrl` — Radarr base URL (empty to disable)
- `prowlarrUrl` — Prowlarr base URL (empty to disable)
- `overseerrUrl` — Overseerr base URL (empty to disable)
- `notebookEnabled` / `notebookPath` — persistent notebook configuration

## Test plan
- [x] `go build ./...` passes
- [x] `go test ./...` passes (config parsing tests confirm JSON is accepted)
- [ ] Deploy to NixOS host and verify service starts with generated config

{"body":"## Summary
- Replace fragile manual YAML string concatenation in `nix/module.nix` with `builtins.toJSON`, eliminating indentation bugs like the recent telegram fields fix
- Add missing NixOS module options for radarr, prowlarr, overseerr, and notebook to match the Go config struct
- Use `lib.optionalAttrs` for all conditional config sections (telegram, radarr, prowlarr, overseerr, notebook)

## Details

The Go binary uses `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` which parses JSON natively (YAML is a superset of JSON), so no Go-side changes are needed. The config file extension stays as `config.yaml` for clarity.

New module options added:
- `radarrUrl` — Radarr base URL (empty to disable)
- `prowlarrUrl` — Prowlarr base URL (empty to disable)
- `overseerrUrl` — Overseerr base URL (empty to disable)
- `notebookEnabled` / `notebookPath` — persistent notebook configuration

## Test plan
- [x] `go build ./...` passes
- [x] `go test ./...` passes (config parsing tests confirm JSON is accepted)
- [ ] Deploy to NixOS host and verify service starts with generated config
